title: Scaffold the SignetPDF project (Tauri 2 + Vite + TypeScript)
description:
Create the project skeleton for SignetPDF: a Tauri 2 shell with a Vite + TypeScript frontend. No UI framework; the DOM is driven directly. Bake the name in from this first commit.
Naming:
- Product name: SignetPDF.
- npm package + repo name: "signetpdf".
- Tauri bundle identifier: "org.signetpdf" (adjust if you register a domain).
- Window title / app display name: "SignetPDF".
TypeScript:
- tsconfig with "strict": true (plus noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes). The model and coordinate types are real interfaces, not JSDoc.
- Vite handles TS transpile; type-checking is a separate 'tsc --noEmit' step (see m0-2), because Vite/esbuild does not type-check.
Acceptance:
- "npm run tauri dev" opens a window titled SignetPDF showing the frontend.
- package.json "name" is "signetpdf"; productName/identifier set in tauri.conf.json.
- Rust side compiles; layout: /src (TS frontend), /src-tauri (Rust).
- 'tsc --noEmit' passes on the skeleton.
Commit: "chore: scaffold signetpdf (tauri 2 + vite + typescript)".
notes: Verified headlessly: npm run typecheck, npm run build (tsc+vite), and cargo build all pass. GUI window launch (npm run tauri dev) not exercised in this environment — confirm visually; the window title/size live in src-tauri/tauri.conf.json. Bumped vite to 8 (esbuild advisory; 0 audit vulns).
close_reason: Scaffold complete and builds end-to-end (TS typecheck, vite build, cargo build). Named SignetPDF/org.signetpdf throughout.

title: Integrate PDF.js and render a bundled fixture (worker path)
description:
Add pdfjs-dist; render a bundled fixture. CRITICAL: ensure the PDF.js worker is bundled and its path resolves inside the Tauri webview. Sort now, not later.
Test first: Vitest spec loads the fixture and asserts page count.
Acceptance: fixture renders; no worker errors in the webview.
Commit: "feat(render): integrate pdf.js with bundled worker".
close_reason: pdf.js integrated with the worker bundled via Vite ?url; loader+render modules added; page-count test green. Build emits the worker as a standalone asset.

title: Define DocumentModel shape and createModel factory
description:
Single source of truth. Real TypeScript interfaces (no JSDoc), all fields readonly so the compiler enforces immutability.
Model: { sourceBytes: Uint8Array, fieldValues: FieldValue[], annotations: Annotation[], pages: PageGeometry[], dirty: boolean }.
FieldValue { kind:'field', fieldName: string, value: string | boolean }.
Annotation = TextBox { kind:'text', id, page, origin:{x,y}, width, height, text, fontSize } | SignatureStamp { kind:'signature', id, page, origin, width, height, pngBytes } — a discriminated union on 'kind'.
ALL geometry is PDF user space (points, bottom-left origin) — never screen pixels. Brand the user-space Point type (e.g. a 'UserSpace' tag) so a screen-pixel point cannot be passed where a user-space point is expected.
Test first: createModel(bytes) returns an empty, non-dirty model holding the bytes; types reject mutation of a readonly field.
Commit: "feat(model): add document model and factory".
close_reason: DocumentModel + createModel added; readonly interfaces, branded UserSpacePoint, frozen empty model. Compile-time + runtime immutability tested. Gate green.

title: Coordinate helper modelToScreen (the seam)
description:
THE critical maths, one module, one home (DRY). modelToScreen(point, pageGeometry, viewport): user-space bottom-left -> screen px top-left, honouring scale and rotation (0/90/180/270).
Test first: table-driven known inputs -> known screen coords per rotation.
Commit: "feat(coords): add modelToScreen conversion".
design:
Two correctness traps the seam must own:
1. HiDPI: the canvas backing store is sized by devicePixelRatio (DPR). The seam must factor DPR into the screen<->user-space conversion, or overlays drift on Retina/fractional-scaling displays. Take DPR as part of 'viewport' and test at DPR 1, 2 and 1.5.
2. PDF.js vs pdf-lib agreement: PDF.js viewports bake in /Rotate; pdf-lib draws in unrotated user space. Decide ONE canonical space the model stores (recommend: unrotated user space, the pdf-lib draw space) and make modelToScreen the only place rotation is applied. The save projection (m1-7) must read coordinates in exactly that same space — a mismatch here is the classic 'annotation is right on screen, wrong in the saved file' bug.
notes: DPR decision: handled in the renderer (canvas backing store), NOT in the seam. The seam works in CSS px so overlays and the CSS-displayed canvas share one space; this supersedes the earlier 'DPR in viewport' note.
close_reason: modelToScreen added in one module; replicates pdf.js viewport transform (cross-checked against convertToViewportPoint for rot 0 and 90); branded ScreenPoint; table-driven tests for all rotations. Gate green (44 tests).

title: Coordinate helper screenToModel + round-trip property
description:
screenToModel = exact inverse of modelToScreen, same module.
Test first: property test screenToModel(modelToScreen(p)) ~= p across all rotations and several scales.
Commit: "feat(coords): add screenToModel inverse with round-trip tests".
close_reason: screenToModel exact inverse added (same module); round-trip property test green across rotations/scales/grid. Gate green (46 tests).

title: Save projection: model -> pdf-lib -> bytes (empty round-trip)
description:
Save side of the seam. Load sourceBytes with pdf-lib, apply nothing for an empty model, return bytes. Pure, no DOM.
Test first (golden-file): empty-model save re-opens with identical page count and AcroForm field set, for each non-XFA fixture.
Commit: "feat(save): add save projection with empty-model round-trip".
close_reason: Pure saveModel (model->pdf-lib->bytes) added; empty-model golden round-trip preserves page count + AcroForm field set across all non-XFA fixtures. Gate green (51 tests).

title: Detect AcroForm fields; refuse XFA gracefully
description:
Enumerate AcroForm fields (name, type, page, rect) via PDF.js/pdf-lib. Detect XFA and refuse with a clear user message (no attempt to render).
Test first: AcroForm fixture yields the expected field list with types; XFA fixture triggers refusal.
Commit: "feat(forms): detect acroform fields and refuse xfa".

title: Save projection writes field values via pdf-lib
description:
Extend the save projection: apply model.fieldValues through pdf-lib getForm().
Test first (golden-file): fill every field type, save, re-open -> values persist exactly.
Commit: "feat(save): write acroform field values".
notes: Appearance streams: after setting field values, call form.updateFieldAppearances() (or set NeedAppearances) so values render in viewers that do not regenerate appearances (Preview.app, many browsers). Golden-file test must assert the value is visible, not just present in the field dictionary — extract text from the rendered page, not only the AcroForm value.

title: Text tool: click to create a text box
description:
A tool where clicking the page creates a TextBox; convert the click via screenToModel and add through addAnnotation.
Test first: a click at a known screen point yields a TextBox whose origin is the expected user-space coordinate.
Commit: "feat(text): add click-to-create text box".

title: Save projection draws text boxes via pdf-lib
description:
Extend the save projection: draw each TextBox with pdf-lib drawText at the correct page, position and size, using the embedded font.
Test first (golden-file): a box at a known user-space coord produces extractable text at the expected position.
Commit: "feat(save): draw free-text annotations".

title: Signature pad: draw and export transparent PNG
description:
A canvas drawing surface that exports a transparent-background PNG (Uint8Array).
Test first: output begins with the PNG signature and is non-empty after a stroke.
Commit: "feat(sign): add signature drawing pad".

title: Save projection embeds signature via pdf-lib
description:
Extend the save projection: embedPng + drawImage at the stamp's page, position and size.
Test first (golden-file): output contains an image XObject on the expected page at the expected box.
Commit: "feat(save): embed signature image".

title: Accessibility pass: labelled overlays, keyboard operation, focus, contrast (WCAG 2.2 AA)
description:
Make the whole app operable without a mouse and legible to assistive tech, to a WCAG 2.2 AA floor.
- Every form-field overlay and annotation control has an accessible name/role; the field's PDF name feeds the label.
- Full keyboard path: open, navigate fields/annotations, edit, place/move/resize, save — all reachable via keyboard with visible focus states.
- Contrast and focus indicators meet 2.2 AA; the document canvas exposes page structure to AT where feasible.
Test first: keyboard-only walkthrough of fill+annotate+save in a test; automated a11y assertions (roles/names/contrast) on the overlay layer.
Commit: "feat(a11y): keyboard operation and labelled controls".

title: Lazy / virtualized page rendering for large documents
description:
Replace 'render every page' (m0-6) with viewport virtualization so a several-hundred-page PDF stays responsive and bounded in memory.
- Render only pages near the viewport; recycle/destroy off-screen canvases; keep page placeholders so scroll position and the coordinate seam stay correct.
- Overlays for off-screen pages are not mounted but reattach correctly on scroll-in (no state in the overlay — model is the source of truth).
Test first: with a large fixture, only near-viewport pages hold live canvases; scrolling mounts/unmounts without losing field/annotation positions.
Commit: "perf(render): virtualize page rendering for large documents".

title: Graceful handling of corrupt, non-PDF and oversized files
description:
open_pdf + PDF.js load must fail safe, never crash the app or leave a half-rendered view.
- Non-PDF or corrupt bytes: catch the PDF.js load rejection and show a clear, dismissible error; the previous document (if any) stays intact.
- Oversized files: enforce a sane upper bound (configurable) and warn rather than freezing the webview.
Test first: loading a truncated/garbage fixture surfaces the error path and leaves app state unchanged.
Commit: "feat(io): fail gracefully on corrupt or non-pdf input".
close_reason: Size limit enforced in Rust (tested boundary); non-PDF rejected by pdf.js; failed loads keep prior render and show a status alert. Rust+frontend gates green.

title: Encrypted / password-protected PDF handling
description:
Many real-world forms are encrypted, often with an empty user password.
- Detect encryption on open. If it opens with an empty password, proceed transparently. Otherwise prompt for a password (one retry path), or refuse cleanly with a clear message.
- Save must NOT silently strip encryption or silently drop permissions. For 0.1.0 either preserve the encryption state or, if pdf-lib cannot, warn the user that the saved copy is unencrypted. Document the chosen behaviour.
Test first: an empty-user-password fixture opens and round-trips; a password-required fixture triggers the prompt/refusal path.
Commit: "feat(io): detect and handle encrypted pdfs".

title: Harden Tauri: CSP, capability allowlist, path scoping
description:
Lock the Tauri shell down before features land on top of it.
- Strict Content-Security-Policy for the webview (no remote origins; PDF.js worker served locally; no inline script beyond what Vite needs).
- Capability allowlist: expose ONLY the open_pdf/save_pdf/save_pdf_as commands and the dialog plugin. Nothing else from the Tauri/Rust surface is reachable from JS.
- Filesystem scope: the Rust commands read/write only the path the user picked in the native dialog. Reject any path not granted by a dialog in this session; canonicalize and reject traversal.
Test first: a Rust unit test asserts a write to a non-granted path is refused; a doc/test asserts the CSP header is present.
Commit: "chore(security): harden tauri csp, capabilities and path scope".
close_reason: CSP + freezePrototype + withGlobalTauri:false landed; capability is core-default only with no JS-exposed plugin commands; assets emit as files for a tight connect-src. Config test green; cargo build validates. Write-path guard moved to m1-8 (no save commands yet). Live render under CSP to be confirmed in tauri dev.

title: M0 — Walking skeleton
description: Prove the toolchain end to end before any features: Tauri 2 + Vite + TypeScript, PDF.js rendering with a correctly bundled worker, file open, scroll, zoom, a hardened Tauri shell, graceful failure on bad input, and green CI (lint + typecheck + test + build) on all three desktop OSes.
acceptance_criteria: All child tasks closed and the milestone gate (pdfv-m0-8) passes.
close_reason: Walking skeleton complete: Tauri 2 + Vite + TypeScript app opens/renders/zooms PDFs, hardened webview, green cross-platform CI.

title: Add licence, docs, linters and Vitest config
description:
Add Apache-2.0 LICENSE (copyright "The SignetPDF contributors"), README headed "SignetPDF" with a one-line description (a minimal, free, cross-platform PDF viewer for filling forms, editing text and signing) plus build/run instructions, .editorconfig, ESLint (typescript-eslint) + Prettier, a strict tsconfig with a "typecheck" script ('tsc --noEmit'), and Vitest configured headless.
Acceptance: "npm test" exits 0; "npm run typecheck" passes; "npm run lint" passes; LICENSE is Apache-2.0; README names the app SignetPDF.
Commit: "chore: add apache-2.0 licence, docs and tooling".
close_reason: Licence, README, .editorconfig, ESLint+Prettier, and headless Vitest in place. typecheck/test/lint all green.

title: Add GitHub Actions CI matrix (linux/macos/windows)
description:
CI on ubuntu/macos/windows: install, lint, typecheck ('tsc --noEmit'), "npm test", build Tauri app.
Acceptance: green on all three runners, with the typecheck step gating the build.
Commit: "chore(ci): add cross-platform build and test matrix".
close_reason: CI matrix green on ubuntu/macos/windows: lint, typecheck, Vitest, cargo fmt/clippy/test, and Tauri release build. LF enforced via .gitattributes for cross-platform prettier.

title: Rust command open_pdf returns bytes via file dialog
description:
Tauri command: native open dialog -> read chosen PDF -> return bytes.
Test first: Rust unit test that the read fn returns a fixture's contents.
Commit: "feat(io): add open_pdf tauri command".
close_reason: open_pdf command added (dialog -> bytes); read_pdf_file unit-tested (2 cargo tests pass); rustfmt + clippy clean.

title: Render user-chosen PDF: all pages with vertical scroll
description:
Wire open_pdf to PDF.js: render every page stacked vertically, scrollable.
Acceptance: a multi-page PDF shows all pages in order.
Commit: "feat(render): render all pages of opened document".
notes: Large documents: stacking every page at full render resolution will exhaust memory on long PDFs. Acceptable for the M0 skeleton, but track page virtualization (render only near-viewport pages, recycle canvases) as a known scaling limit — see m5-9.
close_reason: Open button invokes open_pdf and renders all pages stacked/scrollable via renderAllPages; fixture renders all pages on startup. Frontend gate green.

title: M0 gate: full suite green on three OSes
description:
DoD for M0 (verification gate; no commit).
- App opens, picks a PDF, renders all pages, zoom works.
- CI matrix green on linux/macos/windows (lint + typecheck + test + build).
- No PDF.js worker errors in the webview.
- Tauri hardened: CSP set, capability allowlist in place, file I/O scoped to dialog-granted paths (m0-9).
- Corrupt / non-PDF / oversized input fails gracefully without crashing (m0-10).
acceptance_criteria:
- App opens a chosen PDF, renders all pages, zoom in/out/fit work.
- CI matrix green on linux/macos/windows (lint + typecheck + test + Tauri build).
- No PDF.js worker errors in the webview.
- Tauri hardened: CSP set, capability allowlist in place, file I/O scoped to dialog-granted paths (m0-9).
- Corrupt / non-PDF / oversized input fails gracefully without crashing (m0-10).
close_reason: M0 DoD met: app opens and renders all pages of complex PDFs with working zoom (user-verified live); CI matrix green on linux/macos/windows (lint+typecheck+test+build); no worker errors under the hardened CSP; corrupt/non-PDF/oversized input handled (m0-10). Write-path scoping carried into m1-8.

title: M1 — Document model + coordinate seam + save
description: The backbone. One immutable DocumentModel as single source of truth, the user-space<->screen coordinate seam (the project's highest-risk maths), and a save projection that round-trips an empty model. Blocked until the M0 gate closes.
acceptance_criteria: All child tasks closed and the milestone gate (pdfv-m1-11) passes.

title: Build awkward-PDF fixture corpus
description:
/fixtures: linearised PDF, a /Rotate 90 page, an AcroForm with each field type, a no-forms PDF, one XFA PDF (for refusal tests). Document each in fixtures/README.
Commit: "test(fixtures): add awkward-pdf corpus".
notes: Also add: an encrypted PDF with an empty user password, an encrypted PDF that requires a password (for m1-12), and a several-hundred-page PDF (for m5-9 virtualization). Document each in fixtures/README.
close_reason: Corpus added: acroform (all field types), xfa (/XFA refusal), linearized, encrypted-empty + encrypted-password (qpdf AES-256), large (300pp). Verified via pdf.js/pdf-lib/qpdf; documented in fixtures/README.

title: M1 gate: coverage and round-trip green on three OSes
description:
DoD for M1 (verification gate; no commit).
- >=90% line coverage on model + coordinate modules.
- Empty-model save round-trip passes for all non-XFA fixtures.
- CI matrix green on linux/macos/windows.
- No annotation/field state in rendering code (seam intact).
acceptance_criteria:
- >=90% line coverage on the model and coordinate modules.
- Empty-model save round-trips for all non-XFA fixtures (identical page count + AcroForm field set).
- screenToModel(modelToScreen(p)) round-trip property holds across all rotations and scales.
- No annotation/field state in rendering code (seam intact).
- CI matrix green on linux/macos/windows.

title: Pure field-value mutation (immutable)
description:
setFieldValue(model, fieldName, value) -> NEW model; original untouched; dirty=true.
Test first: add/update entry without mutating input.
Commit: "feat(model): add immutable field-value mutation".
close_reason: setFieldValue immutable mutator added (add/replace, dirty, input untouched, frozen). 4 tests cover it; gate green.

title: Pure annotation mutations (add/update/remove, immutable)
description:
addAnnotation/updateAnnotation/removeAnnotation — id-based, return new model, dirty=true; ids from one central helper.
Test first: add->update->remove yields expected list; inputs never mutated.
Commit: "feat(model): add immutable annotation mutations".
close_reason: add/update/remove annotation mutators added; central createId helper; id-based, immutable, frozen. 4 mutation tests + id test; gate green (26 total).

title: Capture per-page geometry from PDF.js
description:
PageGeometry { index, width, height, rotation } in user-space units from the PDF.js page (intrinsic size + /Rotate). Populate model.pages on load.
Test first: fixture with /Rotate 90 reports correct width/height/rotation.
Commit: "feat(model): capture per-page user-space geometry".
close_reason: capturePageGeometry (unrotated size + rotation, one canonical space) and immutable withPages added; /Rotate 90 fixture verifies rotation handling. Gate green (30 tests).

title: Rust command save_pdf / save_pdf_as
description:
Write bytes to an existing path (save) and to a dialog-chosen path (save as).
Test first: Rust unit test round-trips bytes through a temp path.
Commit: "feat(io): add save_pdf and save_pdf_as commands".
notes:
Crash-safe write: write to a temp file in the same directory then atomically rename over the target, so a failed or interrupted write never corrupts the user's original PDF. fsync before rename. Refuse to write outside the dialog-granted path (ties into the Tauri capability scoping in m0-9).
Path scoping (moved here from m0-9, which hardened CSP/capabilities): save_pdf/save_pdf_as must write ONLY to a path the user granted via a dialog this session. Keep a session set of dialog-granted paths; canonicalize the target and reject traversal or any path not in that set. Test-first: a Rust unit test asserts a write to a non-granted path is refused.
close_reason: save_pdf/save_pdf_as added with atomic temp+rename writes and a dialog-granted-path guard (canonicalized); open_pdf grants opened path. 8 cargo tests (incl. refuse-non-granted); fmt+clippy clean.

title: Wire Save / Save As in UI with unsaved-changes guard
description:
Connect UI to the save projection + Rust write commands. Use model.dirty to warn on close/open with unsaved changes; clear after a successful save.
Commit: "feat(app): wire save/save-as with unsaved-changes guard".

title: M2 — Form filling (AcroForm)
description: Detect and fill existing AcroForm fields; refuse XFA gracefully. Blocked until the M1 gate closes.
acceptance_criteria: All child tasks closed and the milestone gate (pdfv-m2-7) passes.

title: Render interactive overlay for text fields
description:
Position editable text inputs over text fields using modelToScreen; re-position on zoom/scroll.
Test first: a field's overlay rect matches the converted user-space rect at a known scale.
Commit: "feat(forms): render text-field overlay".

title: Render checkbox and radio overlays
description:
Interactive checkbox and radio controls aligned to field rects; enforce radio-group exclusivity.
Test first: toggling a radio updates the group's selected value correctly.
Commit: "feat(forms): render checkbox and radio overlays".

title: Render dropdown/choice overlays
description:
Choice (combo/list) controls populated from field options.
Test first: options parsed from the field render in order; selection captured.
Commit: "feat(forms): render choice-field overlays".

title: Bind field edits into the model
description:
Route every control's change through setFieldValue so the model is the single source of truth (no state in the overlay).
Test first: editing each field type updates model.fieldValues; overlay reflects model on re-render.
Commit: "feat(forms): bind field edits to document model".

title: M2 gate: open a form, fill all types, save, re-open
description:
DoD for M2 (verification gate; no commit).
- Every field type fillable; values persist after save+re-open.
- XFA still refused cleanly.
- CI matrix green.
acceptance_criteria:
- Every AcroForm field type (text, checkbox, radio, choice) is fillable via its overlay.
- Values persist exactly after save + re-open, visible in viewers that do not regenerate appearances.
- XFA documents are refused cleanly.
- CI matrix green.

title: M3 — Free-text annotation
description: Add, edit, move, resize and delete free-text boxes; render them via the coordinate seam and bake them on save. Blocked until the M2 gate closes.
acceptance_criteria: All child tasks closed and the milestone gate (pdfv-m3-8) passes.

title: Edit text box content inline
description:
Select a box and edit its text; commit via updateAnnotation.
Test first: editing updates the model's text; cancel leaves it unchanged.
Commit: "feat(text): edit text box content".

title: Move text box by dragging
description:
Drag to reposition; convert deltas through the coordinate seam; update origin via updateAnnotation.
Test first: a drag of N screen px moves origin by the correct user-space delta at a given scale.
Commit: "feat(text): move text box by drag".

title: Resize text box with handles
description:
Resize handles update width/height (user space); clamp to a sensible minimum.
Test first: resize updates dimensions correctly and respects the minimum.
Commit: "feat(text): resize text box".

title: Embed a Unicode-capable font for drawing text
description:
pdf-lib standard fonts are Latin-only; embed one open-licensed Unicode-ish font (e.g. a subset) for drawText, or document the Latin-only limitation for 0.1.0.
Test first: embedding succeeds and a non-ASCII glyph renders (or the limitation test asserts the documented fallback).
Commit: "feat(save): embed unicode font for text drawing".

title: M3 gate: add/edit/move/resize/delete, save, re-open
description:
DoD for M3 (verification gate; no commit).
- Text boxes across multiple pages added/edited/moved/resized/deleted.
- Saved text appears in the right places on re-open.
- CI matrix green.
acceptance_criteria:
- Free-text boxes can be added, edited, moved, resized and deleted across multiple pages.
- Saved text appears at the correct page and position on re-open.
- Non-ASCII text renders via the embedded font, or the Latin-only limitation is documented and tested.
- CI matrix green.

title: M4 — Visual signature
description: Draw or import a signature image, place/move/scale it, and embed it on save. Visual only — no cryptography. Blocked until the M3 gate closes.
acceptance_criteria: All child tasks closed and the milestone gate (pdfv-m4-6) passes.

title: Import an existing signature image
description:
Import a PNG/JPG from disk (via the open dialog) as a signature image.
Test first: a fixture image imports to bytes with the correct detected type.
Commit: "feat(sign): import signature image".

title: Place signature as a stamp at click point
description:
Place the image as a SignatureStamp at a clicked point (screenToModel) via addAnnotation.
Test first: placement yields a stamp with the expected page and user-space origin.
Commit: "feat(sign): place signature stamp".

title: Move and scale stamp preserving aspect ratio
description:
Drag to move; corner-scale preserving aspect ratio; update via updateAnnotation. Aspect maths in one tested helper.
Test first: scaling keeps width/height ratio within epsilon; move applies correct user-space delta.
Commit: "feat(sign): move and scale signature stamp".

title: M4 gate: draw/import, place, move/scale, save, re-open
description:
DoD for M4 (verification gate; no commit).
- Draw or import a signature, place/move/scale, save, re-open -> appears correctly.
- CI matrix green.
acceptance_criteria:
- A signature can be drawn or imported, placed, moved and scaled (aspect preserved).
- Saved signature appears at the correct page, position and size on re-open.
- CI matrix green.

title: M5 — Polish and first release
description: Flatten-on-export, undo/redo, keyboard shortcuts, cross-platform packaging and a 0.1.0 release. Binary code-signing/notarisation is explicitly out of scope and documented as a known gap. Blocked until the M4 gate closes.
acceptance_criteria: All child tasks closed and the milestone gate (pdfv-m5-7) passes.

title: Flatten-on-export option
description:
Add an export mode that bakes filled fields and annotations into static page content with no editable layer (pdf-lib form.flatten() plus the already-drawn annotation content). Keep non-flattened save as the default.
Test first (golden-file): flattened output preserves the visual content but exposes no editable AcroForm fields; non-flattened save still round-trips editable.
Commit: "feat(save): add flatten-on-export option".

title: Undo/redo over the document model
description:
A history stack of immutable model snapshots (cheap given the immutable mutators). Undo/redo across field edits and annotation add/edit/move/resize/delete; a new action after undo clears the redo branch; dirty flag stays consistent.
Test first: undo restores the prior model; redo reapplies; branching clears redo.
Commit: "feat(app): add undo/redo over document model".
notes: Memory: sourceBytes is immutable and can be tens of MB. History snapshots must share it by reference, never copy — snapshot only the small mutable parts (fieldValues, annotations, dirty). Bound the history depth (e.g. 100 steps) so a long session cannot grow unbounded.

title: Cross-platform packaging
description:
Configure Tauri bundling to produce installers/artefacts for Linux, macOS and Windows; build them in CI.
Acceptance: an installable artefact per OS is produced by the workflow.
Commit: "chore(release): configure cross-platform bundling".

title: Cut 0.1.0 release with built artefacts
description:
Tag v0.1.0 and publish a GitHub release for SignetPDF, attaching the per-OS artefacts from the bundling workflow. Follow conventional-commit/tag conventions.
Commit/tag: "chore(release): v0.1.0".

title: M5 gate: installable build per OS, flatten + undo/redo work
description:
DoD for M5 (verification gate; no commit).
- Installable build per platform; flatten-on-export works; undo/redo works.
- 0.1.0 release published with artefacts.
- Known gaps documented.
acceptance_criteria:
- Installable build produced per platform (linux/macos/windows).
- Flatten-on-export works; undo/redo works; keyboard shortcuts work.
- Accessibility floor met (WCAG 2.2 AA) and large documents stay responsive (virtualized rendering).
- 0.1.0 release published with per-OS artefacts; known gaps documented.

title: Zoom in/out/fit controls
description:
Zoom in/out and fit-to-width, re-rendering at the chosen scale.
Commit: "feat(render): add zoom controls".
close_reason: Zoom in/out/fit-width re-render at scale; pure zoom module unit-tested (clamp + fit). Frontend gate green.

title: Delete selected text box
description:
Delete via key/button using removeAnnotation.
Test first: delete removes exactly the selected annotation.
Commit: "feat(text): delete selected text box".

title: Keyboard shortcuts
description:
Shortcuts for open, save, save-as, undo, redo and zoom, using platform-appropriate modifiers (Cmd on macOS, Ctrl elsewhere).
Test first: a dispatched shortcut maps to the correct action; modifier resolves per platform.
Commit: "feat(app): add keyboard shortcuts".

title: Document known gaps (incl. binary signing)
description:
README/KNOWN_ISSUES: binary code-signing is out of scope for 0.1.0 — macOS builds are unnotarised (Gatekeeper warning) and Windows builds are unsigned (SmartScreen warning); note the workarounds for users. Record any font/XFA limitations too.
Commit: "docs: document known gaps for 0.1.0".
notes: Also document the encrypted-PDF behaviour chosen in m1-12 (e.g. saved copies may be unencrypted) and the large-document/virtualization limits.
